zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Houghton於1449年出生在英國藍卡爾霍格頓時，他的父親Sir Henry Hoghton28歲，母親Lady Helen Mosson20歲。他和Mary Southworth至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1501年8月18日在英國藍卡爾普雷斯頓享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Houghton was born in 1449, in Hoghton, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir Henry Hoghton, was 28 and his mother, Lady Helen Mosson, was 20. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Mary Southworth. He died on 18 August 1501, in Preston, Lancashire, England, at the age of 52.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Houghton(이)가 1449년 영국 랭카실르 호그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry Hoghton(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Helen Mosson(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Mary Southworth(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1501년 8월 18일 영국 랭카실르 프레스턴에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
